findings.summary: USER-REPORTED (operator, 2026-07-22): PUT /agents/providers/anthropic/credential returns 422 'Anthropic credential not recognized. Console API keys start with sk-ant-api. Claude Code O...' — operator cannot save an Anthropic credential. Dual-mode validation (sk-ant-api03-/sk-ant-oat01-, Phase-7 DEF-A d2df452) is rejecting the operator's real credential.
reproduction: Login as operator on production
Agents screen → Anthropic provider → paste credential → Save
Observe 422 with 'not recognized' detail
findings.expected: A genuine current Anthropic credential (Console API key OR Claude Code OAuth token, current prefix formats) saves successfully (200), verifies, and routes bare claude-* runs to direct Anthropic billing
findings.observed: 422 'Anthropic credential not recognized...' on save
findings.verifiedBy: qa-adversary (opus) LIVE prod @d105031, 2026-07-22T04:13-04:15Z; adversarial/ML-agents-cred-001-prodverify.md; server-log corroborated; state restored
findings.rca: agents.py:2110-2121 accepts only sk-ant-api / sk-ant-oat01- prefixes, no whitespace strip, 422 text hardcodes oat01. Operator on-disk token sk-ant-oat01- (128ch) but EXPIRED 2026-07-21; pasted value evidently matched neither prefix (newer-prefix or unicode-whitespace hypothesis). No regression since d2df452.
findings.planReview: PASS-WITH-AMENDMENTS (catalog/ML-agents-cred-001-plan-review.md): anchor prefix to ^sk-ant-oat\d+- in BOTH agents.py:2119 + llm_client.py:470 (NOT bare sk-ant-oat — would admit legacy sk-ant-oat- subscription-oauth shape); keep 'sk-ant-oat01-' literal example in 422 (test_gap_p7_def_a_dual_mode.py:168); add compliance-guard test {authMode:oauth_token, sk-ant-oat-LEGACY} must still 422.
findings.reviewVerdict: PASS (diff review /tmp .../ML-cred-001-diff-review.md): compliance hole closed (^sk-ant-oat\d+- both files), secret hygiene clean, scope tight, tests load-bearing, 14/14+5/5 re-run green independently.

findings.summary: OPERATOR-MANDATED (2026-07-22): the Anthropic credential / configure-credentials window must OPEN THE ANTHROPIC AUTH WEB PAGE for subscription users (in-app-initiated OAuth authorization, Claude Code-style PKCE), not require manual token pasting. Manual paste stays as honest fallback for Console API keys.
reproduction: Agents screen → Anthropic provider → configure credentials
Observe: only manual paste fields; no button that opens Anthropic's authorization page
findings.expected: Subscription mode shows a 'Connect Anthropic / open authorization page' action that launches Anthropic's OAuth authorize URL (PKCE); user approves on Anthropic's page; resulting authorization code is exchanged for an oauth token (sk-ant-oat01-*) stored in the vault with authMode oauth_token; runs on bare claude-* models then bill to the subscription via Bearer + anthropic-beta oauth header
findings.observed: Paste-only dual-mode fields (Phase-7 DEF-A); no in-app auth-page launch
findings.owner: reviewer (diff review) → deploy → qa-adversary prod verify
findings.verifiedBy: qa-adversary(opus) LIVE prod @61fa6bf: start issues valid PKCE authorize URL (distinct challenge+state, DB state row), authorize→claude.ai Pro/Max subscription login 200, exchange rejects 9 bogus inputs (no fake 200, single-use blocked, 0 stored), Connect UI works, regression OpenRouter 337+fit-scorer 200. adversarial/ML-agents-cred-002-prodverify.md. findings.blueprint: docs/delivery/ML-agents-cred-002-BLUEPRINT.md + catalog/ML-agents-cred-002-cli-oauth-extract.md; ADR-ML-2 rulings. Flow=manual code-paste-back+backend exchange (client 9d1c250a, authorize claude.com/cai/oauth/authorize, token platform.claude.com/v1/oauth/token, scope user:inference, PKCE S256, state single-use). Storage repos exist (no DDL). Auto-refresh REQUIRED. New endpoints /agents/providers/anthropic/oauth/{start,exchange}. Manual paste retained as fallback. HUMAN-GATED: operator live consent click.
findings.implNote: 7dd17a9: new anthropic_oauth.py + /oauth/{start,exchange,refresh} + refresh hook (llm_client resolve_user_credential) + Connect-with-Anthropic UI. 26/26+5/5, 114 regression slice green. Token store: used→ProviderCredential(deployment-wide), refresh→AnthropicOAuthToken. Operator live consent HUMAN-GATED.
findings.reviewVerdict: core PASS + delta PASS (both fresh reviewers). PKCE/state-single-use/secret-hygiene/no-split-brain/refresh-honest/exchange-honest/resolve_provider-unchanged/authorize=subscription/needs_reauth-honest/reconnect-wired all verified. 116 backend + 76 FE green.

findings.summary: ADJUDICATED (PIPELINE-RCA.md): the '71% failure' is LARGELY a §3.4-SWEEP ARTIFACT — the DB has 1 real user (operator) whose OpenRouter override was churned through ~13 models today incl. deliberately-broken ones by my own no-substitution sweep; 3/11 cover failures fall in that window. NOT a regression; claude-sonnet-4/direct-Anthropic NEVER invoked (phantom-ignore works, 100% via OpenRouter). Downgraded HIGH→MED. GENUINE underlying bug = cover _draft() (cover_letter_agent.py:1290) has no LLMUnavailableError resilience + complete_json no-retry (see ML-pipeline-001). Fix those.
reproduction: GET /agents/catalog on production; observe coverLetter status/last_run
Run cover-letter pipeline live from UI with seeded story+JD; classify outcome (full success / graceful degrade / hard error)
findings.expected: Cover-letter agent EITHER produces a real business letter OR degrades gracefully (coverLetterUnavailable) — never a hard error state; honest UI
findings.observed: catalog shows coverLetter ERROR state / last run failed 02:49:45Z
findings.verifiedBy: LIVE smoke @25200ce (batch3-smoke.md, 5/5 PASS) + review PASS + 86-test suite: '71%' was §3.4-sweep artifact (config-verified-clean); genuine bug (cover _draft resilience) fixed — live cover run degrades honestly + refunds, no raw leak.
findings.configCheck: agent-config-state.md: operator agent configs CLEAN — 0 pointing at broken/denylisted models (5 deterministic, 3 phantom claude-sonnet-4 ignored→OpenRouter default, storyExtraction claude-haiku-4-5 ignored→qwen STRUCTURED). Confirms no ongoing broken-model failures; the high rates were sweep-only.
findings.reviewVerdict: PASS (ML-pipeline-diff-review.md): retry bounded+honest (_MALFORMED_JSON_RETRIES=1), ADR-ML-3 preserved, async degrade refund-once, draft resilient, email no-op zero-cost billing-unchanged, FE matcher reachable, rebase integrity + batch-2 green. 86 backend +21 vitest +build. 2 cosmetic non-blocking.

findings.summary: §3.2 GAP: model picker is PROVIDER-GLOBAL (single #openrouter-model-picker saving via updateProvider/AgentProvider.model), NOT per-agent. §3.2.1/§3.2.3 require a per-agent picker for ALL agents; 'No agent may be left on an invisible/unchangeable model' and 'selected model persists per agent'. Backend per-agent plumbing exists (AgentConfig.model + _user_model_override agents.py:878) but FE does not render/save per-agent.
reproduction: /dashboard/agents → observe one shared model picker, not one per agent card
Confirm save targets provider (AgentProvider.model) not per-agent (AgentConfig.model via PUT /agents/config/{key})
findings.expected: Per-agent searchable model picker on each agent; selection saved via PUT /api/agents/config/{agent_key}, persisted per agent, used on that agent's next run
findings.observed: Single global OpenRouter picker; per-agent selection not exposed in UI

findings.summary: KNOWN-ENVIRONMENTAL (not a code defect): full-repo pytest shows ~50 non-deterministic failures due to ~35 stale prior-run worktrees sharing the single aether_test Postgres schema (concurrent TRUNCATE races — documented flakiness pattern). Proven NOT caused by cred-001: same failing tests pass in isolation against BOTH pre-fix 9396bae and post-fix code; 0 of the 50 files reference the touched functions.
reproduction: Run full backend pytest while other worktrees/sessions run pytest → non-deterministic failures
Re-run the same failing tests in isolation → pass
findings.expected: G-07: a fresh full-suite run is green with counts logged
findings.observed: 50/1025 fail under concurrent shared-schema load; 0 fail in isolation
findings.owner: G-07 stage: clean stale worktrees + serialized fresh full run
findings.note: At G-07: git worktree prune + remove stale worktree-agent-*/fix-mv-* worktrees, ensure no concurrent pytest, run full pytest+vitest+Playwright fresh under flock, log counts. Any residual failure then is a REAL finding.
findings.gatePlan: G-07 PREREQ (gate-prep 2026-07-22): 33 stale worktree-agent-* worktrees + 83 stale local branches (fix/mv-*, fix/nf-*, prior runs) cause the shared aether_test contention. CLEANUP at G-07 (AFTER all fix agents finish): git worktree prune + remove all non-main worktrees; git branch -D the merged/abandoned locals; then run full pytest+vitest+Playwright fresh (serialized, no concurrent pytest) + log counts. G-08 ALREADY MET: git ls-remote origin = refs/heads/main only; gh pr list = 0 open PRs.

findings.summary: Non-blocking (reviewer note): after deleting the anthropic credential, a stale AnthropicOAuthToken row with needs_reauth can make the provider show 'warning' instead of 'unconfigured' in a narrow self-inflicted delete-then-reread sequence (pre-existing DELETE behavior — the delete path does not also clear the oauth token row).
reproduction: Connect anthropic OAuth → force needs_reauth → DELETE credential → re-read provider shows 'warning' not 'unconfigured'
findings.expected: After credential delete, provider status returns to 'unconfigured' (oauth token row cleared/ignored)
findings.observed: Shows 'warning' (stale needs_reauth row) in the narrow sequence
findings.reviewVerdict: PASS (ML-cred-cleanup-diff-review.md, revert-verified): upstream_status 422-vs-502 (honest detail, network still 502, refresh untouched), needs_reauth gated on source!=none (delete→unconfigured, live-cred still warning), 48/48. Batch-4+5 combined deploy.

findings.summary: EVIDENCE-INTEGRITY: runtime monitor-tail.sh perl regex broken — unescaped '/' in HTTP/1\.1 closes the perl /.../ match delimiter, perl exits rc=255 per line, live-matches.log can never capture. Prior continuous-capture 'monitor clean' claims rest on a broken instrument. (System IS clean — independently confirmed by direct log-greps + 17/17 route-sweeps, a SEPARATE working mechanism — but the capture leg must be fixed for G-06.)
reproduction: Run matches_error() from monitor-tail.sh on a line containing 'ERROR' → NO-MATCH + rc=255
Control m{} delimiter → matches
findings.expected: Continuous capture appends matching ERROR/5xx/Traceback lines to live-matches.log
findings.observed: perl rc=255 every line (delimiter broken); live-matches.log always 0 lines
findings.note: Fail-before rc=255 proven; pass-after 11/21 correct matches; capture restarted (new PID); retro-triage last 3h direct-grep = 0 missed real signatures (system genuinely clean, only instrument broken). FINAL verification = G-06 live observation window must show the working monitor capturing a clean window. Independent capture re-check before G-06.

findings.summary: Cloudflare edge replaces the app's honest JSON 502 body ({detail: 'Anthropic rejected the authorization code — restart Connect with Anthropic.'}) with a generic 'error code: 502' page on the OAuth exchange-failure path, so the operator loses the actionable retry message (400/422 JSON pass through fine). Honesty intact (502 pinned, nothing stored). Fix: map the upstream-rejection to a 4xx so the JSON detail survives the edge.
reproduction: POST /oauth/exchange with real state + fake code → app POSTs token endpoint, gets 400 → returns 502 → Cloudflare masks detail
findings.expected: Operator sees the actionable JSON detail (map to 4xx e.g. 422)
findings.observed: Generic 'error code: 502' text page from Cloudflare
findings.owner: fixer-medium (batch with post-FIX-1, same anthropic_oauth.py file)
findings.reviewVerdict: PASS (ML-cred-cleanup-diff-review.md, revert-verified): upstream_status 422-vs-502 (honest detail, network still 502, refresh untouched), needs_reauth gated on source!=none (delete→unconfigured, live-cred still warning), 48/48. Batch-4+5 combined deploy.

findings.summary: §3.4.4 SILENT MODEL SUBSTITUTION: llm_client _auto/_model_chain catches ANY live failure (incl. 404 stale/unavailable model id) and silently retries a hardcoded FALLBACK_MODEL (openai/gpt-oss-20b:free — different model/provider), returning SUCCESS with no error surfaced. A user's chosen model that fails at run time is silently swapped. Pre-existing; surfaced by FIX-1's honesty claim.
reproduction: Set a user-chosen model on an agent that 404s at run time
Run the agent → observe success on gpt-oss-20b:free, no honest error, no refund
findings.expected: §3.4.4: a USER-CHOSEN model that errors at run time surfaces an honest user-readable error + refunds quota; NEVER a silent fallback to a different model
findings.observed: Silent substitution to FALLBACK_MODEL with fake success
findings.verifiedBy: §3.4 NO-SUBSTITUTION-PROOF.md (20/20 real failures honest+refund, 0 substitution) + Agents deep-test (REASONING-tier emailAgent honors override). No-silent-substitution BLOCKER PROVEN live for overridable agents.

findings.summary: §3.4.3(c): catalog offers OpenRouter models with NO live endpoint for this key (genuine upstream 404 at run time) as equally-selectable, not flagged/filtered 'unavailable'. Run-time error IS honest+refunded (no billing harm) but §3.4 requires unavailable ids be filtered from the catalog OR flagged 'unavailable' in UI. Observed: allenai/olmo-3-32b-think, inflection/inflection-3-pi.
reproduction: Select allenai/olmo-3-32b-think (valid catalog id) → run → upstream 404 'No endpoints found' → opaque 'AI service unavailable' after a (refunded) reservation

findings.observed: Shown as freely selectable; failure only discovered at run time

findings.expected: n/a — handled correctly
findings.observed: Honest 503+refund (correct behavior)
findings.verifiedBy: INFORMATIONAL — not a defect. The 1/82 transient malformed response on the default deepseek-v4-pro was handled correctly (honest 503 + refund + no silent fallback, per §3.4). Ops-monitoring note only; no code fix. Accepted.

findings.summary: UNSURE (dashboard-display tester): Networking has a working backend DELETE endpoint for contacts but NO UI delete control. Could be intentional (contacts not user-deletable via UI) or a minor affordance gap. Adjudicate at Stage-3.
reproduction: /dashboard/networking → add a contact → no visible delete/remove control though backend DELETE works
findings.expected: Either a UI delete affordance, or intentional-by-design (documented)
findings.observed: No UI delete; backend endpoint exists
findings.verifiedBy: ORCHESTRATOR ADJUDICATION: not a defect. Networking is a lightweight contact tracker; contact-delete via UI is a nice-to-have affordance, not a correctness/honesty issue (no placeholder, no broken wiring — the backend DELETE exists and works, verified by the dashboard-display tester's create+delete lifecycle). Accepted as-design; surfacing a UI delete is an OPTIONAL future enhancement, out of scope for this run's zero-tolerance (which targets defects/placeholders/honesty, not missing nice-to-haves).

findings.summary: GET /api/billing/entitlement intermittently 500s (2/4 fresh signups) on a BRAND-NEW user's first dashboard load right after /signup → frontend fails-closed showing 'We could not verify your subscription' instead of the paywall. Isolated to first-ever entitlement checks (0/5 for existing admin). Likely non-atomic INSERT...WHERE NOT EXISTS race in ensure_user_billing() billing.py:~256 vs Subscription/UsageQuota unique indexes on userId.
reproduction: POST /signup new user
Immediately load /dashboard → GET /api/billing/entitlement occasionally 500 → 'could not verify subscription'
findings.expected: First entitlement check ALWAYS succeeds — provisioning is atomic/idempotent (ON CONFLICT / IntegrityError-safe); new user sees paywall (or entitlement) honestly, never a 500
findings.observed: Intermittent 500 on first-ever entitlement check for new users
findings.verifiedBy: evidence(haiku) LIVE prod @fb8361e: 6/6 fresh signups entitlement 200 (0×500), concurrent stress all-200 + exactly 1 sub/quota each, logs clean, users cleaned. adversarial/ML-signup-001-prodverify.md.
findings.rca: billing.py:268-290 ensure_user_billing._run — INSERT...WHERE NOT EXISTS (non-atomic) vs Subscription/UsageQuota unique(userId) → UniqueViolation → GET /entitlement:782 500 → subscription-gate.tsx:231 fallback. Fix = ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING.
findings.reviewVerdict: PASS (ML-signup-001-diff-review.md): ON CONFLICT target correct (unconditional unique indexes), semantics preserved, no suppression, idempotent, scope 1 file, tests load-bearing (pre-fix swap → 500 reproduced), 60/60.
findings.deployNote: Batch-1 live @fb8361e: entitlement 200 (deployer-verified). qa prod-verify (6 fresh signups + concurrent) in flight.

findings.summary: storyExtraction shows a FUNCTIONAL model picker but the pick is a silent no-op — saves 200 yet the run always uses the fixed qwen/qwen3-coder-next (STRUCTURED tier is deliberately non-overridable per design, to protect JSON). Dishonest UI: implies the pick takes effect. The ML-catalog-008 N2 lock covered only DETERMINISTIC backends; non-overridable LLM tiers (STRUCTURED) were missed. Control (emailAgent, REASONING) correctly honors overrides.
reproduction: /dashboard/agents → storyExtraction → pick a model → save 200 → run → served model = qwen/qwen3-coder-next regardless (repro'd twice)
findings.expected: Non-overridable agents (deterministic OR STRUCTURED-tier) show the honest 'fixed model / not user-selectable' lock — no functional picker that silently no-ops
findings.observed: storyExtraction shows a functional picker; pick ignored at run time
findings.owner: test-author → fixer-hard (extend lock to non-overridable tiers)
findings.verifiedBy: screen-tester(sonnet) LIVE prod-verify @5eb8a36 (batch2-prodverify.md, verified twice): storyExtraction STRUCTURED shows honest lock w/ real fixed model qwen/qwen3-coder-next; REASONING agents keep functional pickers; deterministic locked.

Batch-2.

findings.summary: ADJUDICATED (probe ML-agents-003-oauth-url-probe.md): the current claude.com/cai/oauth/authorize (scope user:inference) is the CORRECT SUBSCRIPTION flow (mints sk-ant-oat01- billed to Max). platform.claude.com/oauth/authorize is the API-KEY-creation flow — switching would BREAK subscription billing. CF challenge on claude.ai is Anthropic's own bot-protection (real-operator-passable), NOT an app defect. Downgraded HIGH→LOW: resolution = KEEP URL + add an honest UI hint ('you may see a security check — that's Anthropic's login') to reduce operator confusion.
reproduction: Agents → Anthropic → Connect with Anthropic → opens claude.com/cai/oauth/authorize → claude.ai/login shows Cloudflare challenge
findings.expected: Correct subscription authorize flow (kept) + optional honest CF-expectation hint
findings.observed: CF-challenged claude.ai login (subscription flow); platform.claude.com is cleaner but may be wrong billing flow
findings.owner: fixer (fold hint into Agents re-fix cluster)
findings.verifiedBy: screen-tester(sonnet) LIVE prod-verify @5eb8a36 (batch2-prodverify.md, verified twice): CF security hint present; authorize URL unchanged (claude.com/cai). ADR-ML-5.

findings.summary: The FabricationError graceful-degrade path exists only in the pipeline runner, NOT the single-agent ASYNC path the Cover Letter Studio actually uses → raw exception text leaks to the user, and the frontend's dedicated rejection panel can structurally never fire (it matches 422 only, but the async failures are always 502).
reproduction: Cover Letter Studio → generate → on FabricationError, async path returns 502 with raw exception; FE rejection panel (422-matcher) never shows
findings.expected: The async single-agent cover path degrades gracefully like the pipeline runner (honest coverLetterUnavailable / rejection panel), no raw exception leak
findings.observed: Raw exception, 502, rejection panel never fires
findings.verifiedBy: LIVE smoke @25200ce (batch3-smoke.md, 5/5 PASS) + review PASS + 86-test suite: cover async run degraded HONESTLY (coverLetterUnavailable message), quotaRefundedAt set, costUsd=0, no raw exception/class-prefix leak/hang.
findings.rca: Pipeline path degrades gracefully agents.py:1558-1600; async single-agent path workers/tasks.py:262 does NOT; FE hardcodes failed async→502 api/agents.ts:92-97 while rejection matcher requires 422 rejection.ts:74 → structurally unreachable. Fix: async path graceful-degrade + align FE matcher to the async failure shape.
findings.reviewVerdict: PASS (ML-pipeline-diff-review.md): retry bounded+honest (_MALFORMED_JSON_RETRIES=1), ADR-ML-3 preserved, async degrade refund-once, draft resilient, email no-op zero-cost billing-unchanged, FE matcher reachable, rebase integrity + batch-2 green. 86 backend +21 vitest +build. 2 cosmetic non-blocking.

findings.verifiedBy: LIVE smoke @25200ce (batch3-smoke.md, 5/5 PASS) + review PASS + 86-test suite: no stuck-hang — degrade completes + surfaces honestly (resolved transitively by cover-002 async completion).

findings.summary: GENUINE (RCA): complete_json() (llm_client.py:1343-1366) never retries a malformed-JSON response — the JSON parse happens OUTSIDE _auto()'s retry loop, so even the default model chain hard-fails on one malformed response instead of retrying. A bounded malformed-JSON retry would materially improve tailor/cover/structured reliability (all agents benefit).
reproduction: A structured LLM call returns malformed JSON → complete_json raises immediately, no retry → run fails
findings.expected: Bounded retry (e.g. 1-2x) on malformed JSON before failing honestly
findings.observed: No retry; immediate hard fail on one malformed response
findings.verifiedBy: LIVE smoke @25200ce (batch3-smoke.md, 5/5 PASS) + review PASS + 86-test suite: malformed-JSON retry (bounded, unit-tested 2/2) + live happy-path real generation confirmed (retry didn't break generation).
findings.reviewVerdict: PASS (ML-pipeline-diff-review.md): retry bounded+honest (_MALFORMED_JSON_RETRIES=1), ADR-ML-3 preserved, async degrade refund-once, draft resilient, email no-op zero-cost billing-unchanged, FE matcher reachable, rebase integrity + batch-2 green. 86 backend +21 vitest +build. 2 cosmetic non-blocking.

findings.summary: An oversized adversarial field value is correctly rejected server-side (422) but the FE renders the RAW unwrapped Pydantic error (echoing the full invalid input back), blowing the page to ~50,600px horizontal overflow. Error rendering should be honest+bounded, not raw-echo.
reproduction: /dashboard/settings → paste an oversized value in a field → save → 422 → FE renders raw Pydantic error + full input → ~50,600px overflow
findings.expected: Honest, bounded, user-friendly validation error (no raw Pydantic echo, no page overflow)
findings.observed: Raw Pydantic error echoed + 50,600px overflow
